commands.repair_reason: A focused repair can preserve explicit approval ID registration/resolve semantics without changing the planner architecture.; Needs attention: The diff touches exec authorization and currently drops valid explicit approval resolutions during command analysis, which must be fixed before this security-sensitive path can merge.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve explicit approval resolution during analysis — src/gateway/se... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a planner-backed command authorization path and routes POSIX allowlist, allow-always persistence, approval summaries, control-command rejection, and shell rendering through it.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR regression: source review shows explicit IDs are not registered until after command analysis, and the live head check fails the existing node.invoke approval-bypass test at the approved invoke assertion.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can preserve explicit approval ID registration/resolve semantics without changing the planner architecture.
